data.title: Albertsons gains amid Wells Fargo upgrade on `Plan B' if Kroger deal fails
data.description:
Albertsons (NYSE:ACI) rose 1.5% after Wells Fargo raised its rating to overweight as the firm sees a "compelling value opportunity" as the resolution of its sale to Kroger (KR) nears its conclusion.

"While the company has been bogged down by the 2-year KR M&A saga, resolution either brings a takeout or (more likely) a solid "Plan B" against easy compares and a sizable share repo," Wells Fargo analyst Edward Kelly wrote in a note. Kelly raised his Albertsons (ACI) price target to $23 from $22.

Albertsons (ACI) is likely to have a solid "Plan B" in the event the federal court rules against the planned $25 billion purchase of the supermarket chain, according to Kelly.

"We see upside from Media capture (ACI is well behind KR's $500M+ EBIT biz), shrink improvement (large but stabilizing headwind), pharmacy attachment (growing rapidly), deepening digital/loyalty engagement (recent investment), and strategic rationalization," Kelly added.

Albertsons (ACI) is likely to have $4 billion in share repurchase capacity, representing over 35% of ACI's current $18 billion market cap, if the deal is blocked, including a $400 million termination fee from Kroger (KR).

"The likelihood of KR buying ACI for $27.25 seems low, but isn't zero," Kelly wrote. "As a stand-alone company with the fundamental opportunity and financial flexibility we highlight, we see a reasonable bull case in the mid $20s (high-5x EBITDA)."

If the Kroger (KR) deal breaks, Albertsons (ACI) fundamental floor is $17 a share, Kelly wrote.

On Monday, an RBC analyst said Albertsons (ACI) is likely worth $21 a share if its almost $25 billion sale to Kroger (KR) is blocked by a court after the Federal Trade Commission sued to stop the combination earlier this year.

data.title: Novartis receives positive CHMP opinion for its breast cancer treatment
data.description:
• Novartis (NYSE:NVS) announced that the Committee for Medicinal Products for Human Use of the European Medicines Agency has adopted a positive opinion and recommended granting marketing authorization for Kisqali.
  • Kisqali is used for the adjuvant treatment of adults with hormone receptor-positive/human epidermal growth factor receptor 2-negative early breast cancer, at high risk of disease recurrence, including those with node-negative disease.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Spirit Aero, hurt by Boeing strike, to furlough 700 workers for 21 days - Reuters
data.description:
Spirit AeroSystems (NYSE:SPR) is set to tell employees that it will furlough 700 workers for 21 days, as the strike at Boeing (BA) cuts into the supplier's cash and inventory space, Reuters reported Friday.

The furloughs reportedly will affect Spirit Aero (SPR) employees working on Boeing's (BA) 767 and 777 widebody jets, whose production has been stopped by the strike.

"These furloughs are necessary as we have run out of storage space for 767 and 777," a Spirit Aero (SPR) spokesperson told Reuters, also warning the company would need to lay off workers and announce additional furloughs if the strike continues past November.

Spirit Aero (SPR) also has already fully drawn a $350M bridge term loan facility set up when Boeing (BA) agreed to acquire its former supplier, and it is expected to seek additional help from the planemaker, according to the report.

data.title: Okta in spotlight as Canaccord Genuity cuts price target after Oktane conference
data.description:
• Okta (NASDAQ:OKTA) was in the spotlight on Friday as Canaccord Genuity cut its price target on the cybersecurity company after its annual Oktane conference.
  • Shares were little changed in premarket trading.
  • "Sentiment among customers and partners at the event was somewhat mixed given ongoing seat-based headwinds throughout the customer base and a burgeoning competitive environment, but skewed positive thanks to optimism around new products and initiatives like Interoperability Profile for Secure Identity in the Enterprise and Secure Identity Integrations (initial launch includes 125+ integrations with more to come in March 2025)," analyst Kingsley Crane wrote in note to clients. Crane kept his Hold rating on Okta but lowered his price target to $82 from $90.
  • Crane said that he came away from Oktane "encouraged" by management's strategy to improve its performance, positioning in the market and make every attempt to re-accelerate topline growth. However, "ongoing demand headwinds inform our base case that a full recovery will take more than a few quarters," Crane added.
  • Canaccord Genuity is the second firm to cut its price target on Okta after the event, with Mizuho doing so earlier this week.

data.title: Lilium faces uncertain future without funding from German government - analyst
data.description:
Without a loan from the German government and the matching funds from the State of Bavaria, Lilium N.V. (NASDAQ:LILM) will have to quickly find other ways to raise the cash it needs to avoid insolvency, substantially reduce costs, or move production to either France or China, options which will leave investors cautious until there is better visibility into funding expectations, said Canaccord Genuity analyst Austin Moeller.

The decision by the Green Party in the German Bundestag on Thursday drove Lilium (LILM) shares more than 20% lower and triggered a flurry of downgrades across Wall Street, including a downgrade at Canaccord Genuity, and Cantor Fitzgerald, both of whom are now Neutral on the eVTOL maker.

On Thursday, the German government denied a €50M loan to struggling Lilium (LILM), which would have been matched by the Bavarian government. The company need the funding to develop and certify a seven-seater electric airplane, a technology that members of Germany’s Green party felt was too expensive, had high emissions, and was not useful for energy transition. Accordingly, the loan was denied sending shockwaves through Lilium (LILM) stock on Thursday. And although Lilium (LILM) is still continuing negotiations with the Bavarian government for the matching €50M, this advance was contingent on the primary loan from the German State Development Bank (“KfW”).

“While Lilium indicated in a 6-K filing that they are now negotiating with Bavaria over its €50M commitment, the decision in Berlin brings into question whether the State of Bavaria will release the matching funds that were approved in September. Based on comments made regarding the decision by Bavarian Minister President Markus Soder, this outcome remains unlikely,” Moeller said in his downgrade of Lilium (LILM) to Hold from Buy. Moeller also slashed his target price by more than half to just $0.70.

Amid hopes that Lilium (LILM) will be successful in securing a €50M loan from the State of Bavaria, shares are recouping a fraction of Thursday’s 21% freefall.

data.title: Nasdaq futures higher boosted by Netflix's earnings beat
data.description:
Nasdaq and S&P 500 futures rose on Friday, while Dow futures fell, as Netflix's (NFLX) third quarter earnings beat helped bring cheer to investors.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) +0.3%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) +0.6% and Dow futures (INDU) -0.2%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was down 1 basis point to 4.09%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 1 basis point to 3.96%.

Streaming giant Netflix (NFLX) rose 5% in premarket trade after it beat both its top and bottom line estimates in the third quarter. The firm also topped expectations for user growth.

Wall Street on Thursday scaled a fresh all-time intraday high on a boost from technology stocks and a robust retail sales report that bolstered expectations of a soft landing, but then gave up those gains in the afternoon and eventually ended largely flat.

"The main catalyst for the day's market moves was another batch of upbeat U.S. data, which dampened any immediate fears about a potential recession," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

U.S. data once again reminded investors of the wisdom of never underestimating the hedonism of the US consumer. September retail sales were strong, UBS' Paul Donovan said.

"ECB yesterday for the first time this cycle. Meanwhile, markets priced out the likelihood of the Federal Reserve doing the same at every meeting over the next few months," Reid added.

The European Central Bank cut its policy rates by 25 basis points, as expected, on Thursday, its third cut of the year.

Housing starts and building permits unexpectedly slumped in September.

There are also a few Fed speakers, including Fed Atlanta President Raphael Bostic and Fed Minneapolis President Neel Kashkari.
